method.description: RSS, filtered HTML fallback and optional external JSON collection for Baltic and Poland hybrid threat monitoring.

items.title: Baltic states 'strongly reject' Russian lies in Moscow
items.summary: Diplomats from the Baltic states on Friday strongly rejected disinformation efforts of the Russian Foreign Ministry in Moscow.

items.title: News in simple Latvian: 10th July, 2026
items.summary: Every evening at 21:00, Latvian Radio 1 broadcasts a five-minute summary of the day's news stories in a simplified language format called Ziņas vieglajā valodā ('News in simple language').

items.title: Updated International Strategy to empower the EU Cybersecurity Ecosystem
Cybersecurity Preparedness DIY: Build Your Own Cybersecurity Exercise
Assess your National Cybersecurity Capabilities and Maturity with the updated ENISA Framework
5th consecutive victory for Team Europe, the still undefeated EU Champions of the International Cybersecurity Challenge
Cyber Europe 2026: All eyes on the EU’s collective response and resilience
ENISA to operate the EU Cybersecurity Reserve with EUR 36 million
What’s Driving Cybersecurity Investments and where lie the challenges?
IOC clears way for Russian athletes ahead of 2028 Olympics
A European Perspective Estonia gives Orthodox church 6 months to choose between God and the Kremlin
NATO leaders meet in Ankara as Trump rekindles disputes over Greenland, Iran
Polish deputy PMs hail NATO summit success, say Europe taking greater role in allied defence
Polish court convicts Russian couple over espionage for Moscow
War in Ukraine Ukraine says Russia spread AI-generated deepfake video aimed at sowing Poland-Ukraine discord 10.07.2026 14:00
Business NATO to invest billions in strategic fuel pipeline to Poland
Lithuania condemns move to allow Russian athletes back into international competition
Russian invasion of Ukraine
Lithuaniaâs FM assures Belarus policy remains unchanged despite programme wording
NausÄda receives engraved revolver from Erdogan at NATO summit
LRT English Newsletter: Government pledges and NATO improvs
OSCE report: Russia's treatment of Ukrainian children may amount to crime against humanity
New Estonian ambassador to Japan highlights strategic security ties
items.summary: Estonia's new Ambassador to Japan, Andres Unga, has presented his credentials to His Majesty Emperor Naruhito.

items.title: Kertu Saks: 35 years from restoration of independence and the absence of fear
items.summary: Reflecting on events 35 years after Estonia's re-independence, Kertu Saks argues that Estonia's greatest source of resilience was the quiet resolve to make calm, decisive choices together in the face of uncertainty.

items.title: Estonia's 'Night Fairies' help keep Beach Grind festival-goers safe
items.summary: A nightlife safety group whose main aim is to warn revelers of the dangers of drug use and to aid them where needed is on hand at the ongoing Pärnu Beach Grind festival this weekend.

items.title: New U-Boot flaws could enable stealthy firmware attacks
items.summary: Six vulnerabilities in the widely used U-Boot bootloader have been discovered that could allow attackers to execute malicious code during device boot, potentially enabling stealthy firmware attacks that compromise security protections and install persistent malware. [...]

items.title: Europe revives law allowing big tech to scan for CSAM
items.summary: The law known as Chat Control 2.0 passed in the European Parliament, permitting companies like Google, Meta and Microsoft to scan users' messages to hunt for CSAM.

items.title: Ryuk ransomware member pleads guilty in the US, faces 15 years in prison
items.summary: A 34-year-old Armenian man has pleaded guilty to hacking U.S. companies and deploying the infamous Ryuk ransomware to encrypt their systems. [...]

items.title: Ryuk operator pleads guilty; Blackcat/AlphV conspirator gets nearly 6-year sentence
items.summary: One man accused of deploying Ryuk ransomware pleaded guilty Wednesday in an Oregon federal court to conspiracy and computer fraud, while another man received a 70-month federal prison sentence in a Florida court for helping the Blackcat/AlphV gang extort multiple victims.

items.title: Police suspects Dutch hackers were involved in Odido breach
items.summary: The Dutch National Police (Politie) says it has found "strong indications" that Dutch hackers have been involved in a February breach at the telecommunications provider Odido. [...]

items.title: Progress urges ShareFile admins to shut down servers over “credible” threat
items.summary: Progress Software is emailing ShareFile customers who use Storage Zone Controllers to immediately shut down their servers after identifying what it describes as a "credible external security threat" targeting the on-premises secure file-sharing software. [...]

items.title: Hackers exploit critical auth bypass in Gitea Docker image
items.summary: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the official Docker image for the Gitea self-hosted Git service that allows attackers to impersonate any user, including administrators. [...]

items.title: The Replicant in Your Directory: AI Agents and the Identity Security Gap
items.summary: AI agents are accelerating the growth of non-human identities, making it harder for organizations to understand what exists, who owns it, and what it can access. Netwrix explains why stronger visibility and identity governance are essential as AI expands the enterprise attack surface. [...]

items.title: No decision made on LMT/Tet ownership issues
items.summary: In-depth negotiations are currently under way with no fewer than 23 investors regarding potential involvement in the purchase of shares of LMT and Tet from the Swedish telecommunications company Telia. This was stated by Economics Minister Viktors Valainis (Greens and Farmers' Union, ZZS) after an extraordinary government meeting convened specifically for this issue on July 10th.

items.title: Medical professionals underestimate parents' role in alleviating newborns' pain
items.summary: A recent doctoral dissertation found that parents — one of the most effective sources of pain relief for infants — are still not routinely involved during painful procedures.

items.title: Latvia's foreign trade figures were on the up in May
items.summary: Central Statistical Bureau provisional data show that in May 2026 Latvia had foreign trade turnover of 3.86 billion euro, and at current prices it was 8.4 % higher than a year ago.

items.title: Climate law update: no obligations for private sector, only for the state
items.summary: Amendments to the Climate Resilient Economy Act reached the parliament Environment Committee on Thursday after the first reading. Under the changes, obligations to reduce carbon emissions would apply only to the public sector. The opposition says the bill remains empty and without substance.

items.title: China, India ran separate spying campaigns against same Pakistani police force
items.summary: The activity, in some cases breaching the exact same systems, ran between February 2024 and April 2026 and centered on the force responsible for the country’s southwestern province that has been the site of a long-running separatist insurgency.

items.title: Obesity rise slows in wealthier nations, Estonia faces growing challenges
items.summary: An international study reveals a slowdown in obesity growth among affluent countries, while Estonia grapples with rising childhood overweight rates, highlighting urgent public health concerns.

items.title: Estonia's regulation to hide beneficial owners postponed
items.summary: The planned concealment of companies' beneficial ownership data, which was due to take effect on Friday, has been postponed, the Ministry of Finance said.

items.title: Lihtsad uudised 10. juulil
items.summary: "Lihtsad uudised," meaning easy or simple news, is for anyone who wants to improve their Estonian language skills. Listen and read a selection of this week's news stories below.

items.title: Vulnerability in BitWizard mtr software
items.summary: Out-of-bounds read vulnerability (CVE-2026-14461) has been found in BitWizard mtr software.

items.title: Estonian farmers have been hit hard by rising fertilizer prices
items.summary: Farmers are in a difficult situation because fertilizer prices have surged due to the Middle East crisis. The European Union plans to use €540 million from the agricultural crisis reserve to support farmers. Estonia, however, believes that the distribution of funds leaves local producers at a disadvantage.

items.title: Where to watch the climax of the FIFA world cup
items.summary: We are now into the quarterfinals stages of the 2026 FIFA World Cup in North America, and many people wanting to see games with a crowd and atmosphere will be heading to pubs, bars and other venues in Estonia.

items.title: Vulnerabilities in R-SOFT DMS software
items.summary: CERT Polska has received a report about 5 vulnerabilities (from CVE-2026-41876 to CVE-2026-41880) found in R-SOFT DMS software.

items.title: Defense Intelligence: Russia may face air force fuel problems after Ukrainian strikes
items.summary: The Omsk oil refinery, which in previous years produced about 15 percent of the jet fuel Russia needs, has halted operations, said Colonel Ants Kiviselg, head of the Estonian Defense Intelligence Center.

items.title: Exports rise 9 percent in May as trade deficit narrows
items.summary: Exports of goods from Estonia increased by 9 percent year on year in May, Statistics Estonia reported.

items.title: Scientists say dependence on support hindering Estonia's housing fund renovation
items.summary: A recent review found that government renovation subsidies have so far been used more frequently in areas with higher property values, while many apartment associations are reluctant to undertake renovations on their own.

items.title: Nearly 3,000 basic‑school graduates still without a confirmed study place
items.summary: There are currently more than 4,100 available places in upper secondary schools and vocational institutions across Estonia.

items.title: Pedestrian killed after Tallinn tram driver used phone on duty
items.summary: A man was killed after being struck by a Tallinn tram whose driver was using her smartphone while on duty, Õhtuleht reported.

items.title: Viktor Turkin: Children neglected when equal parenting becomes a goal in itself
items.summary: While a child has the right to both parents and both parents have the right and responsibility to participate in raising their child, problems appear when a sound principle becomes an end in itself, Viktor Turkin writes.

items.title: Former ransomware negotiator gets 4 years for BlackCat attacks
items.summary: A former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint was sentenced to 70 months in prison for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]

items.title: Lithuanian arrested in big cigarette seizure in Rīga
items.summary: Tax and Customs Police officers, in cooperation with State Border Guard officers, conducted an inspection of a warehouse in Rīga, in the Teika suburb, at the end of June this year. During the search, a large quantity of cigarettes, vehicles, tracking devices and other items were seized, indicating an intention to covertly transport a large quantity of cigarettes of illegal origin.

items.title: 'Lion's Leap' military exercises taking place this weekend
items.summary: From July 11 to 12, the international military exercise “Lions Leap” will be held in the vicinity of Ozolaine, Vecsaule parish, Bauska district, in which the 53rd Infantry Battalion of the 1st Riga Brigade of the National Guard, the 503rd Infantry Company of the Lithuanian National Guard, and soldiers of the 2025 draft of the State Defense Service will participate.

items.title: Latvia celebrates 50th heart transplant
items.summary: The 50th heart transplant in Latvia has taken place at Pauls Stradiņš Clinical University Hospital (PSKUS), reports Latvian Television.

items.title: Peeter Kaldre: The time of Europe began in Ankara
items.summary: The NATO summit in Ankara may go down in history for the decision that Europe will ultimately take over the alliance's future leadership role from the United States, Peeter Kaldre writes.

items.title: Rīga introduces management fees for municipal apartments
items.summary: From this month, Rīga will start collecting management fees for municipality-owned apartments.

items.title: Wind turbine loses blade during test run in Latvia
items.summary: A loud noise, reminiscent of thunder, shook the Karosta neighbourhood in Liepāja on Wednesday evening. Although strong winds were battering the city in Kurzeme, the cause this time was different: during equipment testing at the Rapsoil wind farm, the blade of one turbine snapped and fell. The rotor is located on the outskirts of the city; no one was injured, TV Kurzeme reports.

items.title: NATO Ankara summit gift leaves Estonian prime minister with revolver dilemma
items.summary: Prime Minister Kristen Michal (Reform) came from this week's NATO summit in Ankara bearing an unusual gift — a revolver which he has had to leave behind in Turkey, Delfi reported.

items.title: Raul Aron: The most important issue has been forgotten in the tax debate
items.summary: The goal of the tax debate should not be to raise taxes until Estonia reaches the European average tax burden, but to ensure a tax system that is as competitive as possible in financing public services, Raul Aron writes. This brings more money into the country and increases tax revenue.

items.title: Dentists who do not speak Estonian causing problems for healthcare system
items.summary: Just under 100 dentists have joined Estonia's workforce in recent years without being required to pass a separate Estonian language exam before starting work. However, this has created problems both for patients and in medical documentation.

items.title: Three for the Weekend: Festivals abound
items.summary: Which festival will you choose for this weekend?

items.title: Latvia is Belarus' first target for hybrid war: Border Guard chief
items.summary: Latvia is currently the number one target of Belarus’s hybrid warfare, and the challenges are immense, Guntis Pujāts, commander of the State Border Guard, told Latvian Television on July 9 regarding the situation on the eastern border.

items.title: News in simple Latvian: 9th July, 2026
items.summary: Every evening at 21:00, Latvian Radio 1 broadcasts a five-minute summary of the day's news stories in a simplified language format called Ziņas vieglajā valodā ('News in simple language').

items.title: Chancellor: Requiring resident permit applicants to submit bank statements unlawful
items.summary: Chancellor of Justice Ülle Madise says it is unlawful for the Police and Border Guard Board (PPA) to require residence permit applicants to submit bank account statements covering the previous two years.

items.title: Artificial intelligence used in Estonian shops' video surveillance systems
items.summary: In some stores across Estonia, security camera footage is also monitored by artificial intelligence, which analyzes shoppers' behavior patterns. However, the technology is not yet widely used.

items.title: Dutch police trace Odido telco cyberattack to suspected local accomplice
items.summary: Dutch police said Thursday they had uncovered evidence suggesting that Dutch criminals were involved in the cyberattack on telecom provider Odido that exposed the personal data of more than 6 million customers earlier this year.

items.title: OpenMandriva Linux says contributor tried to sabotage the project
items.summary: The OpenMandriva Linux project announced that it was the target of an attempted act of internal sabotage after a dispute among contributors. [...]

items.title: Injective SDK on npm infected with cryptocurrency wallet stealer
items.summary: Hackers compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and used it to publish a malicious package on the Node Package Manager (npm) that stole cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed phrases. [...]

items.title: New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
items.summary: A new data-extortion group called Helix is using identity-focused tactics such as voice phishing (vishing), device code phishing, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments. [...]

items.title: University enrolment begins in Latvia Thursday
items.summary: On Thursday, July 9, the unified admissions process for undergraduate programs began at eight Latvian universities. Online applications for admission will continue on the “Latvija.lv” portal from Thursday through July 20, while prospective students will be able to apply in person starting July 14, Latvian Radio reports.

items.title: New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
items.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 focuses on stealing Microsoft 365 accounts by combining adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) and device code methods with AI-assisted lure generation. [...]

items.title: Staged robbery uncovered in Ogre, Latvia
items.summary: The State Police have uncovered a staged robbery in Ogre that was organised to conceal the embezzlement of company funds, police said on July 9.

items.title: Organic dairy brand Bryunaļa discontinued in Latvia
items.summary: The agricultural services cooperative “Viļāni” has a wide range of organic dairy products and the “Bryunaļa” brand, which was launched 11 years ago. Due to the unprofitability of the production facility, a decision was made to discontinue the production of organic dairy products as of July 10, Latvian Radio reported on July 9.

items.title: EU opens infringement case against Estonia over work‑conditions directive
items.summary: The European Commission has opened an infringement procedure against Estonia over the directive on transparent working conditions. The Ministry of Economic Affairs says the issue concerns technical matters that have already been resolved.

items.title: Estonian government approves extending temporary protection of Ukrainian refugees
items.summary: The government on Thursday approved Estonia's position on a draft implementing decision of the Council of the European Union that would extend temporary protection for Ukrainian war refugees through 2028.

items.title: Estonian firm proposes 200 MW gas plant in Viljandi County
items.summary: A company linked to the Evecon energy group has applied to build a 200‑megawatt gas‑fired power plant in Viljandi County, a project that could take years to clear planning hurdles.

items.title: Man drives luxury cars, steals fuel and sandwiches
items.summary: Police officers in Pārdaugava arrested a young man who, since this spring, had stolen fuel from at least six gas stations in Rīga and the surrounding area using luxury cars. The suspect is currently being held in temporary custody, according to the State Police.

items.title: Rain threatens Estonian cherry harvest quality despite bumper crop
items.summary: Stone fruit, cherries in particular, are yielding average to above-average crops this year following 2025's poor harvest, yet the persistent rain is still spoiling the fruit.

items.title: Estonia seeks EU funding ban for IOC over Russia decision
items.summary: Estonia has called for the International Olympic Committee (IOC) to be barred from receiving European Union funding over its stance on Russia competing in top level sport.

items.title: Election candidate removed due to lack of Latvian citizenship
items.summary: The Central Election Commission (CVK) decided on Thursday to remove Jurijs Jesipenko from the list of candidates for the 15th Saeima election in the Rīga electoral district submitted by the political party alliance “Harmony Centre,” the CVK told the LETA news agency.

items.title: PM Kulbergs rates his cabinet '7 out of 10' after first month in office
items.summary: The government of Prime Minister Andris Kulbergs (United List) has fully completed three of a total of 61 priority tasks within a month, he announced at a press conference after a meeting to discuss the government's actions so far.

items.title: History: Early land laws helped bring order to Estonia's fragmented farms
items.summary: In the early 20th century, in newly independent Estonia, farm plots were fragmented and property relations often existed only verbally.

items.title: 'Key figures on Europe' 2026 edition now available
items.summary: On July 9th Eurostat has released the 2026 edition of its Key figures on Europe.

items.title: Tallinn eyes EU funds and investors for €150–200 million Linnahall project
items.summary: Tallinn wants to secure European Union structural‑fund financing for the renovation of Linnahall while simultaneously seeking investors with proven interest, Mayor Peeter Raudsepp (Isamaa) said. Decisions about the building's future must be made in the autumn.

items.title: Latvia's macroeconomic forecasts revised by Finance Ministry
items.summary: The Ministry of Finance (FM) has updated its indicative medium-term macroeconomic and fiscal forecasts, taking into account the latest economic trends and the geopolitical situation. The forecasts indicate slower economic growth and higher inflation than projected in February of this year, the FM reported.

items.title: Estonia still not using €220,000 strawberry fraud detection system
items.summary: Technology developed several years ago to detect imported strawberries falsely marketed as Estonian produce is still not being used, Maaleht reported.

items.title: Vulnerability in SOPlanning software
items.summary: SQL Injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-50644) has been found in SOPlanning software.

items.title: Estonia plans new marine reserves to safeguard reef and bird habitats
items.summary: Estonia has moved to establish the first marine protected areas in its exclusive economic zone, proposing new reserves at the Kolgi and Irbe shoals.

items.title: Lots of lilies coming to Latvian Nature Museum
items.summary: From July 16 to 18, the Latvian National Museum of Natural History will host “Lilies 2026,” a particularly colourful and fragrant exhibition for the gardening season, the museum announced.

items.title: Two minors get prison sentence for double homicide
items.summary: Two minors have been sentenced to 12 years and six months in prison and three years of probation for the murder of two people in Rēzekne, the Latgale District Court has ruled, according to the Prosecutor's Office.

items.title: Central Rīga street sections to be closed until end of July
items.summary: To complete the installation of bike lanes and improvements to street intersections on the section of Dzirnavu Street between Tērbatas Street and Skolas Street, starting Thursday, July 9, through July 30, traffic restrictions will be in place on Dzirnavu Street, according to the Riga City Council.

items.title: Prosecutors appeal justice minister expenses acquittal to Supreme Court
items.summary: The Prosecutor's Office has appealed to the Supreme Court the acquittal of Reform Party MP Kalle Laanet over expenses claims fraud charges, Õhtuleht reported.

items.title: EDF reduces conscript intake for 1 year to focus on training
items.summary: The Estonian Defense Forces will admit 700 conscripts this year, three times fewer than usual, to allow for updated training of professional service members.

items.title: Railway platform modernization project has big problems
items.summary: The project to make Latvia's crumbling railway platforms resemble something from the 21st century is experiencing severe delays, reports Latvian Radio.

items.title: Board‑game enthusiast says players' personalities emerge clearly at the table
items.summary: TalTech game‑club chair Sander Mesila says board games reveal sides of people you don't see elsewhere, calling them one of the best ways to spend time with friends.

items.title: Estonian conscripts learning to use cutting-edge air defense systems
items.summary: Estonia's Air Force's Air Defense Division has relaunched its conscription service after more than 20 years and recruits will learn to use top-of-the-range systems.

items.title: Irja Lutsar: Tallinn hospitals reorganization should avoid half-baked solutions
items.summary: Let's stop experimenting with half-finished solutions and instead develop a long-term, cross-party plan to reorganize Tallinn's hospital network, writes Irja Lutsar.

items.title: Biting midges now rival mosquitoes as Estonia's summer scourge
items.summary: While summer 2026 has been relatively mosquito-free so far, there is a newer blot on the landscape, Maaleht reported: Midges.

items.title: 8 people evacuated after dam floods in Tõrva
items.summary: The Tikste Dam overflowed in Tõrva, Valga County, flooding residential homes. As a result, the municipality evacuated eight people.

items.title: Baltic air policing mission switches to air defence mission
items.summary: NATO will reassign its longstanding Baltic air policing mission as an air defence mission, according to an agreement reached at a summit of NATO member states' leaders in the Turkish capital, Ankara, reports Latvian Television.

items.title: Vulnerabilities in GNU patch software
items.summary: CERT Polska has received a report about 2 vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-56288 and CVE-2026-56289) found in GNU patch software.

items.title: Estonia slams removal of Nordic combined from Winter Olympics
items.summary: The Estonian Olympic Committee (EOK) has hit out at the removal of the Nordic combined skiing event from the next Winter Olympics, ending a tradition over a century old.

items.title: City of Tartu continues to demand compensation for national care reform expenses
items.summary: The City of Tartu has filed an appeal with the Tallinn Circuit Court, asking the court to order the state to pay approximately €1.6 million to reimburse Tartu for the cost of general care services provided in 2024.

items.title: AI data centre to help Latvian doctors make quicker diagnoses
items.summary: Time is of the essence in diagnosing diseases, including cancer, as well as in selecting the most appropriate treatment methods. Thanks to high-performance computing, what used to take years can now be analysed much more quickly. A new artificial intelligence data server will provide Latvian scientists with this opportunity, Latvian Television reported.

items.title: Latvian Border Guard chief: Migrant smugglers are getting aggressive
items.summary: Migrant smugglers have become more aggressive, and border guards are having to use increasingly aggressive methods to apprehend them, said Guntis Pujāts, commander of the State Border Guard (VRS), in an interview on the Latvian Radio programme “Good Morning” on July 9.

items.title: Erik Gamzejev: The political hodgepodge of Kohtla-Järve's June coup
items.summary: After Max Kaur's removal as mayor, Kohtla-Järve's new governing alliance brings together the Koos party, politicians from Estonia's major parties and figures linked to the late local oligarch Nikolai Ossipenko, Erik Gamzejev finds.

items.title: Latvian SUBmerge to work with industry giant Naval Group
items.summary: On Wednesday, July 8, the Latvian underwater drone manufacturer "SUBmerge Baltic" signed a memorandum of cooperation with the French defence and maritime technology company "Naval Group," Latvian Radio reported on July 8.

items.title: Drones help check power lines in Latvia
items.summary: Work that used to take several hours in some places can now be completed in a matter of minutes. The electricity distribution network "Sadales tīkls" is increasingly using drones to inspect power lines, Latvian Television reported on July 8.

items.title: airBaltic fleet down two planes
items.summary: Latvia's national airline, "airBaltic," has reached a mutual agreement with one of its aircraft lessors regarding the early return of two "Airbus A220-300" aircraft at the end of June this year, Augusts Zilberts, airBaltic’s head of public and political relations, confirmed to the LETA news agency.

items.title: Blue-green algae still a problem at several Harju County swimming sites
items.summary: According to the Health Board's public water safety database, swimming is currently not recommended at Pikakari, Pelguranna, Haabneeme, Harku Lake and Pirita beaches.

items.title: Photos: Baltoscandal festival breaks attendance records in Rakvere
items.summary: This year's Baltoscandal international performing arts festival set its highest attendance record of the past decade.

items.title: New US unit will arrive in Estonia this summer, defense minister confirms
items.summary: The next unit of U.S. forces will arrive in Estonia this summer and rotations will continue during the six-month review process, Minister of Defense Hanno Pevkur (Reform) said after meetings with top US officials at the NATO summit this week.

items.title: Canada to increase troop numbers in Latvia
items.summary: NATO's summit in Türkiye concluded on July 8th, with one of the outcomes being a commitment from Canada to increase the number of troops it has stationed in Latvia as part of NATO's collective defence posture.

items.title: News in simple Latvian: 8th July, 2026
items.summary: Every evening at 21:00, Latvian Radio 1 broadcasts a five-minute summary of the day's news stories in a simplified language format called Ziņas vieglajā valodā ('News in simple language').

items.title: Rīga tourism tax will double next year
items.summary: Starting next year, the tourist tax applicable in Rīga will increase. This was decided by city councillors on Wednesday, July 8th.

items.title: Rīga mayor Kleinbergs survives opposition attempt to oust him
items.summary: Rīga mayor Viesturs Kleinbergs (Progressives) defeated an attempt to force his resignation on Wednesday.

items.title: Young Latvians feel a lot better than their elders
items.summary: Young people in Latvia don't feel as well as most of their European Union peers, but at least they feel a lot better than their elders. Such are some of the conclusions that can be drawn from fresh Eurostat data published on July 7th.

items.title: Latvia's main cancer clinic to be reconstructed over two years
items.summary: On Wednesday, July 8, the Oncology Centre at Riga East University Hospital (RAKUS) was handed over to contractors for full-scale reconstruction, covering 10 floors and an area of more than 23,000 square metres, Latvian Radio reported on August 8.

items.title: Monthly inflation in Latvia at standstill
items.summary: Central Statistical Bureau data published on July 8 show that in June 2026, compared with May 2026, the average level of consumer prices remained unchanged.

items.title: Founder of Latvian emergency service passes away
items.summary: Following a serious illness, Dr Renāte Pupele (June 14, 1953–July 5, 2026), the founder and first director of the State Emergency Medical Service (NMPD) and a healthcare management professional, has passed away, according to a post by the NMPD on Facebook.

items.title: Teacher accused of child sexual abuse in Engure, Latvia
items.summary: The State Police are recommending that a teacher in Engure, Tukums Municipality, be charged with at least 25 incidents of sexual abuse against minors. The suspect, a man born in 1950, is currently in custody, Latvian Television reports on July 8.

items.title: Fuel station takeover deal concluded
items.summary: Fuel station operatpr Virši has completed its takeover of 17 fuel stations owned by Naftimpeks into the company’s network, it said in a release on July 8th.

items.title: Invasive species are encamped in the Latvian capital
items.summary: Rīga can be called the capital of invasive plant species in Latvia. Various alien species are increasingly common in forests, meadows and roadsides, crowding out native plants. To stop the spread of invasive species, the involvement of the entire society and an understanding of why they need to be combated is necessary, reports Latvian Television.

items.title: Edward Lucas: 'There are Europeans who are willing to fight, and Europeans who are not willing to fight'
items.summary: The respected journalist and geopolitical analyst Edward Lucas took time to speak to Latvian Television again on July 7th, giving his take on the current NATO summit and Russia's faltering war in Ukraine.

items.title: Russia indulges in further Baltic deportation fantasies
items.summary: In highly unoriginal comments, Russian Foreign Ministry officials have said that the Baltic states are "playing with fire" by opening up their territory for "hostile actions" against Russia.

items.title: Ten million train passengers in Latvia in first half of 2026
items.summary: In the first half of the year, 10.1 million passengers travelled by train, which is 0.3% – or 32,000 – fewer than during the same period in 2025, according to the passenger train operator Vivi.

items.title: Daugavpils faces mass layoffs
items.summary: Daugavpils has been shaken by news of a large-scale round of layoffs – nearly 300 people at two companies will lose their jobs in the near future, LSM's Latgale editorial reports on June 8.

items.title: Chanterelle season has begun in Latvia
items.summary: Chanterelle season has begun – the first wild chanterelles are already available at markets, and the supply is steadily increasing. Mushroom pickers say there aren’t as many chanterelles this year as there were last year, Latvian Radio reports on July 8.

items.title: Vulnerabilities in OpenIDC liboauth2 software
items.summary: CERT Polska has received a report about 2 vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-54430 and CVE-2026-54431) found in OpenIDC liboauth2 software.

items.title: Vulnerabilities in MyComplianceOffice MCO software
items.summary: CERT Polska has received a report about 8 vulnerabilities (from CVE-2026-53902 to CVE-2026-53909) found in MyComplianceOffice MCO software.

items.title: Vulnerabilities in KTM System e-BOK software
items.summary: CERT Polska has received a report about 4 vulnerabilities (from CVE-2026-35095 to CVE-2026-35098) found in KTM System e-BOK software.

items.title: Vulnerabilities in fzf software
items.summary: CERT Polska has received a report about 2 vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-53432 and CVE-2026-53433) found in fzf software.

items.title: Vulnerabilities in Redeight CMS software
items.summary: CERT Polska has received a report about 3 vulnerabilities (from CVE-2026-53690 to CVE-2026-53692) found in Redeight CMS software.

items.title: Vulnerability in Raytha CMS software
items.summary: SQL Injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-12076) has been found in Raytha CMS software.

items.title: Vulnerability in libxml2 software
items.summary: Stack-based Buffer Overflow vulnerability (CVE-2026-11979) has been found in libxml2 software.

items.title: UNC1151/Ghostwriter phishing campaign targeting Gmail accounts
items.summary: Recently, we have been observing attacks by the UNC1151/Ghostwriter group targeting Gmail accounts. This group has been regularly attacking the mailboxes of Polish citizens for several years, although in the past these attacks focused on other email providers. The techniques used evolve over time, but the core theme of the messages and their objective remain unchanged.

items.title: Autonomous fuzzing process under LLM supervision
items.summary: The CCN project is co-financed by the European Regional Development Fund and the State Budget under the European Funds for Digital Development Programme 2021-2027.
